key: the wall street journal reported this week that inflation hit an annual rate of 4.2% in may, the highest it has been since the covid/esser hike from a few years ago.

author: Chad Aldeman
work: The Inflation Problem for Teachers

key: unlike many private-sector workers, teachers often work under multi-year contracts that lock in salaries well before anyone knows what inflation will be. that can leave them vulnerable to sudden shocks.
quote: Unlike many private-sector workers, teachers often work under multi-year contracts that lock in salaries well before anyone knows what inflation will be. That can leave them vulnerable to sudden shocks.
author: Chad Aldeman
work: The Inflation Problem for Teachers

key: over the long, long-term, the average teacher salary has mostly kept up with inflation, but no more than that. and teacher salaries tend to lag behind inflation increases. once inflation comes down, teacher salaries have a chance to catch up.
quote: Over the long, long-term, the average teacher salary has mostly kept up with inflation, but no more than that. And teacher salaries tend to lag behind inflation increases. Once inflation comes down, teacher salaries have a chance to catch up.
author: Chad Aldeman
work: The Inflation Problem for Teachers

key: starting from left to right, you can see that teacher salaries were not growing very fast in 2016 and 2017. but then the 'red for ed' movement started winning larger salary increases in 2018 and 2019, and those kicked in in 2019 and 2020.
quote: Starting from left to right, you can see that teacher salaries were not growing very fast in 2016 and 2017. But then the 'Red for Ed' movement started winning larger salary increases in 2018 and 2019, and those kicked in in 2019 and 2020.
author: Chad Aldeman
work: The Inflation Problem for Teachers

key: the good news is that teacher salaries have historically caught up once inflation stabilizes. the bad news is that when inflation rises suddenly, teachers often spend months or even years absorbing the hit before salary schedules have a chance to adjust.
quote: The good news is that teacher salaries have historically caught up once inflation stabilizes. The bad news is that when inflation rises suddenly, teachers often spend months or even years absorbing the hit before salary schedules have a chance to adjust.
author: Chad Aldeman
work: The Inflation Problem for Teachers

key: as leaders begin the planning process, they can ask llms to poke holes in it, to identify the underlying assumptions the strategy depends upon, and to reveal potential missteps.

key: don't depend on a single prompt. when the llm provides its output, push against it, probe the assumptions it makes, and dive deeper into the questions at hand. the most useful outputs seldom come in the model's first response.

key: by nature, llms are agreeable and seek to satisfy the user. when you're looking for critique, ask the model to be critical. ask it to serve as a true red team partner, poking holes in every aspect of your plan.

key: in organizations where status is worshipped and overplayed, destructive leaders stay in power for a longer time and typically intensify their bad behavior.

key: high-functioning teams and organizations prevent this by institutionalizing the upward voice.

key: setting individual goals to be achieved in the next calendar year sets expectations for team members and keeps them centered on their personal performance and development. goals are the lifeblood for enhanced performance, and good leaders use them to measure progress.
quote: Setting individual goals to be achieved in the next calendar year sets expectations for team members and keeps them centered on their personal performance and development. Goals are the lifeblood for enhanced performance, and good leaders use them to measure progress.
author: Admired Leadership Field Notes

key: the best goal set stretches team members and pushes them to expand their skills and results. a well-crafted set of goals asks a team member to think beyond what they do now and to make significant progress on issues and outcomes that matter.
quote: The best goal set stretches team members and pushes them to expand their skills and results. A well-crafted set of goals asks a team member to think beyond what they do now and to make significant progress on issues and outcomes that matter.
author: Admired Leadership Field Notes

key: the best leaders ask team members to craft their own goal set with an eye toward three distinct goals. they use what team members propose to begin a dialogue around what it will take to elevate performance. they co-create the final goal set to achieve a higher commitment and focus.
quote: The best leaders ask team members to craft their own goal set with an eye toward three distinct goals. They use what team members propose to begin a dialogue around what it will take to elevate performance. They co-create the final goal set to achieve a higher commitment and focus.
author: Admired Leadership Field Notes

key: outcome goals, as the name implies, are the results team members want to attain. these are the most common goals identified in planning sessions. they are typically measurable and time-bound, which makes it easy to tell whether they have been achieved or not.
quote: Outcome goals, as the name implies, are the results team members want to attain. These are the most common goals identified in planning sessions. They are typically measurable and time-bound, which makes it easy to tell whether they have been achieved or not.
author: Admired Leadership Field Notes

key: in contrast, performance goals are the big actions that lead to outcomes but are within the control of the team member. they typically focus on a standard of performance or how team members will achieve the outcomes they desire.
quote: In contrast, performance goals are the big actions that lead to outcomes but are within the control of the team member. They typically focus on a standard of performance or how team members will achieve the outcomes they desire.
author: Admired Leadership Field Notes

key: process goals are the specific actions and skills that give rise to performance. think steps or behaviors that contribute to progress, making performance and results possible. unlike outcome goals that focus on the end result, process goals focus on the journey and the controllable activities that get people there.
quote: Process goals are the specific actions and skills that give rise to performance. Think steps or behaviors that contribute to progress, making performance and results possible. Unlike outcome goals that focus on the end result, process goals focus on the journey and the controllable activities that get people there.

key: people require all three goal types to make significant progress, whether they are athletes, artists, or team members. without all three goal types, people often fail to make the strides they should.
quote: People require all three goal types to make significant progress, whether they are athletes, artists, or team members. Without all three goal types, people often fail to make the strides they should.

key: a debate about the risk of a new strategy may involve one team member focused on probability, another focused on severity, and yet another concerned with reputation. the discussion soon becomes circular because each person is answering a different question, shaped by their own definition.

key: the more similar two options appear, the harder the decision feels. but the harder the decision feels, the more similar the options actually are. and the more similar the options actually are, the less it matters which one you choose. this is fredkin's paradox.
quote: The more similar two options appear, the harder the decision feels. But the harder the decision feels, the more similar the options actually are. And the more similar the options actually are, the less it matters which one you choose. This is Fredkin's Paradox.
author: Admired Leadership
work: Fredkin's Paradox of Similar Options

key: leaders who find themselves paralysed between two options have already received an important signal: the paralysis is evidence of equivalence. stop debating and pick one. the cost of choosing wrong, when the options are this close, is negligible.
quote: Leaders who find themselves paralysed between two options have already received an important signal: the paralysis is evidence of equivalence. Stop debating and pick one. The cost of choosing wrong, when the options are this close, is negligible.
author: Admired Leadership
work: Fredkin's Paradox of Similar Options

key: the energy that leaders spend agonising over close decisions is the most wasteful energy in leadership. not because the decisions don't matter -- they do -- but because when a decision is genuinely difficult, it is difficult precisely because neither option is clearly better.
quote: The energy that leaders spend agonising over close decisions is the most wasteful energy in leadership. Not because the decisions don't matter -- they do -- but because when a decision is genuinely difficult, it is difficult precisely because neither option is clearly better.
author: Admired Leadership
work: Fredkin's Paradox of Similar Options

key: the paradox has a practical corollary that leaders resist: if you cannot decide, you should probably spend less time deciding, not more. more analysis of two nearly-equivalent options produces more data about two nearly-equivalent options. it rarely reveals a decisive asymmetry.
quote: The paradox has a practical corollary that leaders resist: if you cannot decide, you should probably spend less time deciding, not more. More analysis of two nearly-equivalent options produces more data about two nearly-equivalent options. It rarely reveals a decisive asymmetry.
author: Admired Leadership
work: Fredkin's Paradox of Similar Options

key: apply your scrutiny to the decisions that feel easy, not the ones that feel hard. easy decisions that feel obvious might be hiding a non-obvious cost or risk. hard decisions that feel impossible to resolve have usually already told you what they need to tell you: the options are close. execute.
quote: Apply your scrutiny to the decisions that feel easy, not the ones that feel hard. Easy decisions that feel obvious might be hiding a non-obvious cost or risk.
